title: Christella ensifera
taxonomy: Christella ensifera is classified as a species within the genus Christella of the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a family of ferns. Christella ensifera is further classified within the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. This classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other ferns and places it within the broader group of vascular plants. The family Thelypteridaceae is also known as the marsh fern family, which is a reference to the habitat and characteristics of the plants within this group. Christella ensifera's classification is used to understand its unique characteristics and to group it with other similar species.
uses: Christella ensifera is a species of fern used in traditional medicine, particularly in China and other parts of Asia.
